pots_observed_earliest.description: Light brown/yellowish pot, top-right, containing dark soil.
Dark pot, bottom-left, containing dark soil with white fragments (eggshells).

pots_observed_current.description: Dark pot, central, containing dark soil with white fragments (eggshells) and a small green plant.

new_introductions_interventions.description: An uncatalogued pot present in early images, not part of the registry.
White, irregular fragments introduced into the soil of P2.
A new plant specimen with 3-4 rounded, green leaves introduced into P2.
A rectangular blue object placed on the desk surface.
A small circuit board/sensor with wires, placed on the desk surface.
A white writing instrument placed on the desk surface.

systemic_losses.description: The expected Mexican Mint plant in P2 was absent in images [EARLIEST] through [T-4], indicating a systemic loss or non-presence during that period.
The scale anchor and its associated pot (P3) were not observed in any image.
The uncatalogued P1 pot was removed from the observable biome.

inventory_reconciliation.P2_mexican_mint_registry_status: Systemic Loss (plant absent) from EARLIEST to T-4. New Introduction/Intervention (new plant) from T-1 onwards. Visual confirmation as Mexican Mint is not possible based on current leaf morphology.
inventory_reconciliation.P1_registry_status: Not in registry. Observed as a New Introduction/Intervention (uncatalogued pot). Systemic Loss from T-1 onwards.

P1_occupant.details: Contains dark, dry soil. No plant material visible in early images. Removed from biome by T-1.

P2_occupant.status_T1_to_current: Small green plant with rounded leaves
P2_occupant.health_inference_T1_to_current: Healthy. Leaves are turgid, uniformly green, and show no signs of wilting, discoloration, or pest damage. Soil appears moist.
P2_occupant.details: Initially bare soil with white eggshell fragments. A small plant with 3-4 rounded leaves appeared between T-2 and T-1. The plant's morphology does not strongly suggest Mexican Mint without further detail, but it is robust and well-hydrated.

soil_texture.P1: Appears dry and somewhat clumpy in early images.
soil_texture.P2: Appears dry in early images, then moist from T-1 onwards. Contains visible eggshell fragments throughout.

biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings observed in any pot.
biome_observations.debris_on_desk: Wires and a white cylindrical object are present in early images. From T-1 onwards, a blue book, an electronic component with wires, and a white pen are visible.

temporal_deltas.changes: Minimal. Soil surface in P1 appears slightly less uniform. No significant changes to plant presence or general composition. Lighting appears slightly brighter in T-5.
Very subtle. Soil surface in P1 shows slightly more detail, possibly fine dry organic matter. No plant changes. White cylindrical object appears slightly repositioned.
Complete loss of visual data. Image is entirely black, indicating camera malfunction, power loss, or intentional darkness.
No change. Image remains entirely black.

Dramatic transformation. P1 is no longer visible (Systemic Loss). A small, healthy green plant with rounded leaves has appeared in P2 (New Introduction/Intervention). The soil in P2 now appears moist. New objects (blue book, electronic component, white pen) have been introduced onto the desk surface.
No discernible change. The plant in P2 maintains its size, color, and turgidity. Soil moisture and surrounding objects remain consistent.

visual_health_inference.P2_plant: The plant in P2 is visually healthy, exhibiting vibrant green coloration and turgid leaves. The moist soil suggests adequate hydration.

anomalies.description: Images [T-3] and [T-2] are completely black, representing a significant gap in visual monitoring data.
Between [T-4] and [T-1], there was a significant intervention involving the removal of P1, the introduction of a new plant into P2, and the placement of several new objects on the desk.

vision_report.narrative_description: The observation sequence begins with two pots: an uncatalogued P1 (light brown/yellowish) and P2 (dark, expected to contain Mexican Mint). Both pots initially contained bare, dry soil, with P2 also featuring eggshell fragments. The expected Mexican Mint in P2 was absent, marking a systemic loss. Over the first few days ([EARLIEST] to [T-4]), only minor, subtle changes in soil texture were noted. A significant data interruption occurred for two days ([T-3] and [T-2]), with completely black images. Upon resumption of visual data ([T-1]), the biome had undergone a dramatic transformation. P1 was removed (systemic loss). In P2, a small, healthy-looking green plant with 3-4 rounded leaves was introduced, replacing the bare soil. The soil in P2 now appeared moist. Additionally, new objects—a blue book, an electronic component, and a white pen—were present on the desk surface. The final image ([CURRENT]) shows no change from [T-1], indicating stability in the plant's healthy condition and the overall setup. The newly introduced plant in P2 appears robust and well-hydrated.
